example_scripts.topic: The Giver Who Was Never Thanked
example_scripts.script:
You weren't loved for who you are…
you were loved for how gently you held others together.

Like an umbrella in the storm,
you felt essential when the skies broke—
but no one thanks the shelter
once the sun learns how to stay.

You called it patience,
they called it convenience.

And somewhere between giving and waiting,
you forgot—
even the sky lets go of its colors
every single evening.

Not because it lost them…
but because it knows
they were never meant to stay.

You didn't lose them in a fight.
You lost them in a silence that grew too long.
One day they were your whole world.
The next, just someone you used to know.
And the worst part?
You never got to say goodbye.
Some people leave without leaving — and that's the wound that never closes.

Missing someone who's still alive is the loneliest kind of pain.
Because you can't grieve them properly.
They're out there — living, laughing, breathing.
Just not with you anymore.
And every time you see their name, your chest still tightens.
Some distances aren't measured in miles.
They're measured in what was never said.

They broke your heart.
But here's what they didn't break — your ability to love.
You didn't become cold.
You became careful.
And there's a difference.
Cold people stop feeling. Careful people feel everything — they just choose better.
Your next love won't get a broken version of you.
They'll get a wiser one.

We didn't have a falling out.
We just... stopped showing up for each other.
Little by little.
Until one day I realized — I couldn't remember the last time we laughed together.
Friendships don't always end in arguments.
Sometimes they end in unanswered texts and forgotten plans.
And somehow that's even harder to accept.

You were never too much.
Just too real for people who only needed you in their storms.
They held you close when the rain came.
And forgot you the moment the sky cleared.
But here's your lesson — you are not a crisis hotline.
Start saving your warmth for people who stay when it's sunny.

Not everyone who says I love you means I'll stay.
Some people just mean — I like how you make me feel right now.
You were giving forever to people who were only visiting.
And that's why it hurt.
Not because they left.
But because you thought they'd live there.
Lesson: the right person won't need you to convince them to stay.

There was a time… when hearing someone's voice… meant everything.
Back then… people waited hours… sometimes days… just for a call.
Not because it was easy… but because it mattered.
And now… They silence calls… from the people who care the most.
Maybe… They didn't lose connection… They just stopped valuing it.

Sunday used to feel like the world agreed to pause.
Everyone gathered… not because they were summoned… but because they wanted to be there.
The table wasn't fancy… but every seat was filled.
Now Sundays come and go… and most people spend them alone with a screen.
We have more freedom than ever… and nowhere we truly want to be.

Remember when you could tell someone everything… without saying a word?
A glance across a room used to carry the weight of a whole conversation.
People knew how to look at each other… really look.
Now we stare at glass… and call it connection.
The saddest part… is we don't even notice what we've stopped seeing.

love_example_scripts.script:
To be honest…
you're more than just my favorite person.

You're the thought I reach for
when the day gets too heavy.
The voice I want to hear
before I fall asleep.

I don't know how to explain it to anyone else.
I just know that everything feels quieter
when you're near.

Tag the person who makes the world feel smaller — in the best way. ❤️

You don't even know what you do.

You walk in
and my whole chest just… exhales.
Like something that was held tight
finally remembers how to let go.

I've been looking for that feeling my whole life.
Turns out it had your name.

Send this to the person who is your calm. ❤️
